title: Lotononis sericea
taxonomy: Lotononis sericea is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to southern Africa, where it is commonly found in sandy or rocky areas. The plant is classified as a shrub or small tree, typically growing to a height of 1-3 meters. Lotononis sericea is known for its small, white flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The plant's classification at the genus level is within the monotypic genus Lotononis, making it a unique and distinct species within the Fabaceae family.
uses: Lotononis sericea has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In some cultures, Lotononis sericea is used to treat digestive issues and as a remedy for respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lotononis sericea for medicinal purposes. However, its traditional use suggests potential benefits in treating certain health conditions.

side_effects: Lotononis sericea, also known as the horsefly tree, is reported to have toxic properties that can cause various side effects. Ingesting the plant's seeds or leaves can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The plant's toxic compounds can also cause liver and kidney damage in high doses. intro: Lotononis sericea, also known as the hairy lotononis, is a plant species native to southern Africa, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
